TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi beforefieldinit BlazorAuthenticationChallengeHandler extends System.Object

Docs.summary: Handles authentication challenges for Blazor Server components. Provides functionality for incremental consent and Conditional Access scenarios.
Docs.remarks: This handler is designed specifically for Blazor Server scenarios where authentication challenges need to be initiated from component code. It supports incremental consent (requesting additional scopes) and Conditional Access (handling step-up authentication). Use this in combination with to enable seamless authentication flows in Blazor Server applications.

MemberSignature.Value: public BlazorAuthenticationChallengeHandler (Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.NavigationManager navigation, Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Authorization.AuthenticationStateProvider authenticationStateProvider, Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration configuration);

.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(class Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.NavigationManager navigation, class Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Authorization.AuthenticationStateProvider authenticationStateProvider, class Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration configuration) cil managed
Public Sub New (navigation As NavigationManager, authenticationStateProvider As AuthenticationStateProvider, configuration As IConfiguration)

new Microsoft.Identity.Web.BlazorAuthenticationChallengeHandler : Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.NavigationManager * Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Authorization.AuthenticationStateProvider * Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration -> Microsoft.Identity.Web.BlazorAuthenticationChallengeHandler

Docs.summary: Handles authentication challenges for Blazor Server components. Provides functionality for incremental consent and Conditional Access scenarios.
Docs.remarks: This handler is designed specifically for Blazor Server scenarios where authentication challenges need to be initiated from component code. It supports incremental consent (requesting additional scopes) and Conditional Access (handling step-up authentication). Use this in combination with to enable seamless authentication flows in Blazor Server applications.

MemberSignature.Value: public void ChallengeUser (System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal user, string[]? scopes = default, string? claims = default);

member this.ChallengeUser : System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal * string[] * string -> unit

Docs.summary: Initiates a challenge to authenticate the user or request additional consent.

Docs.summary: Initiates a challenge with scopes from configuration.

Docs.summary: Gets the current user's authentication state.

MemberSignature.Value: public System.Threading.Tasks.Task<bool> HandleExceptionAsync (Exception exception);

Public Function HandleExceptionAsync (exception As Exception) As Task(Of Boolean)
member this.HandleExceptionAsync : Exception -> System.Threading.Tasks.Task<bool>

Docs.summary: Handles exceptions that may require user re-authentication. Returns true if a challenge was initiated, false otherwise.

Docs.summary: Checks if the current user is authenticated.
